comments: The band were call Jack Thighs when they recorded this session but had changed their name to Taurus two days before the broadcast.

sessions.details: recorded 1980-04-30 for Friday Rock Show. First TX 1980-06-06.
Saturday Rock Show Session, TX 5 Dec 1973. BBC Radio 1 Bob Harris Session.

track_listing.track: 'Stallions of the Highway' (747 (Strangers in the Night) 12” single)

track_listing.details: LP-Metal for Muthas Volume II compilation

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Oh hello there. TV on the radio here. Thomas the Vance. And welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
To delight your ears this beautiful summer night we have an archive session from 1973 by Rory Gallagher recorded back in the time when he had a pianist.
Also we have the first ever session by a band called Taurus who until Wednesday were called Jack Thighs.
We've got some new stuff by Roxy, Nugent, Whitesnake and Tees, and some classic cuts by Floyd, Leopard, Hawkwind, Hillage and Aphrodite's Child, among others.
Also tonight, of course, we have our Friday Night Connection, that titillation of the week.
Only three correct answers this week, so it looks like we nearly got you.
We start with Black Sabbath.
For Saxon, on their 12-inch version of 747, that being their current single, you'll find that on the B-side, but only on the 12-inch one.
It was, of course, the live version of Stallions of the Highway.
And I played that especially for Martin Dinoff from Manchester,
from a lady by the name of Maggie,
and her black dog, who live in Ilkley, West Yorkshire.
And before that, Black Sabbath, the Sabs,
from their album Heaven and Hell.
